item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a little bit of a theory/headcanon about this…

So, the containment procedures mention a weird bit about a humanoid appearing:

Once a month, a humanoid entity will appear on the border of Threshold Yuf and inform personnel of any adjustments that must be made to these containment procedures. These instructions are to be followed without question, and the entity is then to be executed using a gunshot to the head.

While the containment procedures are strange, this part in particular stands out as being rather strange.

Another interesting thing is how our poor friend Julius managed to come up with these seemingly perfect containment procedures in just a week. Seems an impossible task, but he achieved it, didn't he?

Well, what if he didn't?

See, what if the ritual he designed isn't for the containment of the object, but containment of Julius Green? See, he complained about not having enough information about what he was containing, so why not contain what he could?

But why would he contain himself, you ask? Because he needed more time. He sort-of tricked O5-6 by providing him with containment procedures but also providing himself with more time to do his job.

What I'm getting at is: the figure that appears is some form of Julius Green getting reborn/appearing as a ghost/etc. to add to the containment procedures. He contained his soul/consciousness with some kind of ritual, that allows him to return in the future to update and perfect the ultimate containment procedures.

I have no clue if this is the actual truth of how this all works or anything but I like it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For a general containment procedure that's meant to contain anything and something unknown, it could only contain physical objects that's smaller than a circle 1 meter in diameter?

This Attendant seems very suspicious, but as the Overseer Council did release these emails I guess he's legit? Unless the entire council has been affected by something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this escalated quickly. I didn't even realize we were approaching light speed until we find out Julius' brain had exploded.

Kind of feel look our poor researcher approached too closely to the sun and, like poor Icarus, got burned. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, who says there even was an anomaly that needed to be contained?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do have a point here, the “pitch black" thing wasn't mentioned at all after scp-5101 was made……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First of all, loved it, big +1. Very well written. Couldn’t shake a feeling of oppression and being observe. It is quite strange, because the file states that currently it’s used to contain itself. What about the “pitch black” anomaly? Where is it? And why the orders where specifically from 05-6 and not from from all 05. This smells bad. Seem to me that someone messed up. Did 06 commited a mistake and tried to cover its tracks?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always regarded SCP as a form of escapism -an efficient organization with competent bosses, reasonable decisions, eagerness to take action, and zero corporate bullshit is something to dream of.
Then there's this textbook example of a mundane project. Really grinds my gears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article makes me feel frustrated and angry but I guess that's the point? Being put on unreasonable deadlines with no hope of salvation is a deep-seated nightmare of mine so this certainly presses my spoop buttons, but, well, it's a Boss From Hell story that's also a Shoot the Shaggy Dog story, and it's hard not to feel personally victimized by this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, not saying that it doesn't exist, just saying that it's pretty unusual, and also just… it's incongruent with the typical roles found in the Foundation's various departments + committees. Attendant takes on a more servile, semi-religious connotation, which is totally at odds with the Foundation's style of hierarchy. Even if you were to take the definition you gave, why wouldn't you just refer to them as an assistant? The whole thing is off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the buildup this one has— can't help but feel a little bad for Julius, though. My guess (headcanon?) is that O5-6 needed to contain an SCP with info-hazardous properties that was also capable of causing a K-Class scenario, so not only could they not have a lot of researchers working on it nor give Julius specific details, but they needed the procedures as soon as possible in order to prevent something big from happening.

But I'm sure it all comes down to O5-6 simply being an ass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tanhony pls the mystery contest is over and I have no idea what is going on in this SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, Attendants have shown up in other skips before. I can think of SCP-3411, which also features an Attendant. The role is pretty undefined though, with the only real job description being “operative directly serving and answering to an O5 councilperson.”

Like I said in chat, this is relatable in a very macabre and Douglas Adams-style way. I love the cold indifference of the Assistant and what I believe is a reference to the Pattern Screamers. All around a fantastic article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. Something's very wrong here. What the FUCK is the O5 council doing? This isn't cold, but it sure as hell is cruel. Attendant isn't a role I've seen in the context of Foundation staff, which sets off even more alarms. I'm guessing the containment procedures are predicated on the "sacrifice" of Green - there's definitely a connection between the inner circle's spinal fluid and the combusting brain matter that killed Green. Something's either gotten contaminated in the chain of communications and the anomaly is affecting JL, Green or both, or the O5 council is outsourcing some of its more specialized + less pleasant duties to… unusual sources.

Either way, a delightful and twisted read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to see most of my concerns were alleviated. You've done it again, Tanhony. +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Brewsterion 1017, Jakdragon, Laneous, TrustyOlValet2, Bench, Soulless and DrAkimoto for their valuable feedback!
